answer_fields.adam_beta2: not stated as 0.999 in the cited Adam passages
answer_fields.conflict_resolution: scope/methodology difference: Adam passages define β2 without naming a 0.999 default, while the Transformer paper specifies β2 = 0.98 as its own chosen value; the cited evidence does not establish a contradiction nor an explicit acknowledgement of deviation between the two papers.

field_values.adam_beta2: not stated as 0.999 in the cited Adam passages

claim_verdicts.reason: All cited Adam passages define β1, β2 ∈ [0,1) as decay-rate hyperparameters but never specify β2 = 0.999 as a default value. The claim is fully supported.
claim_verdicts.verified_text: The Adam paper introduces β1, β2 ∈ [0,1) as the hyperparameters controlling the exponential decay rates of the first- and second-moment moving averages, but the cited passages do not state a recommended default value of β2 = 0.999; that commonly cited default is not asserted by the retrieved Adam passages.

claim_verdicts.reason: ev_c478868f3b1b547e contains the exact quoted text from Section 5.3 Optimizer specifying β1 = 0.9, β2 = 0.98, ε = 10^{-9}. The claim matches the evidence verbatim.
claim_verdicts.verified_text: The Transformer paper ("Attention Is All You Need"), in Section 5.3 Optimizer, explicitly states: "We used the Adam optimizer with β1 = 0.9, β2 = 0.98 and ε = 10^{-9}."

field_values.conflict_resolution: scope/methodology difference: Adam passages define β2 without naming a 0.999 default, while the Transformer paper specifies β2 = 0.98 as its own chosen value; the cited evidence does not establish a contradiction nor an explicit acknowledgement of deviation between the two papers.

claim_verdicts.reason: The cited Transformer passage shows β2 = 0.98 as the paper's own chosen value. Combined with the Adam passages (from claim 1's evidence) which do not assert β2 = 0.999 as a default, the resolution correctly identifies that the "conflict" arises from an external default attribution rather than from any contradiction or explicit deviation statement within the cited papers themselves.
claim_verdicts.verified_text: The retrieved Adam paper passages do not assert β2 = 0.999 as a default; they only define β1, β2 as decay-rate hyperparameters in [0,1). The Transformer paper explicitly chose β2 = 0.98 as a task-specific setting for its Adam optimizer. The "conflict" is therefore not a contradiction within the cited papers: the widely cited β2 = 0.999 default is not grounded in the retrieved Adam passages, and the Transformer paper's β2 = 0.98 is a deliberate, paper-specific choice rather than a deviation from an Adam-stated default. No retrieved passage from either paper contains an explicit acknowledgement of deviation from the other.

title: Resolve the conflict between the commonly cited Adam beta2=0.999 default and the claim that the Transformer paper used beta2=0.98, by retrieving the beta2 values actually reported in the Adam paper and in the Transformer paper, plus any explicit acknowledgement of deviation, and explaining how both can be true.
